name: Ja'far ibn Aqeel
title: Devotee of Aqeel
shortDescription: One of the brave sons of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib who entered the battlefield on Ashura with epic poetry and was martyred.
fullStory:
Ja'far ibn Aqeel was the son of Aqeel ibn Abi Talib and one of the heroes of the Hashemite family. After the event of Karbala, the family of Aqeel presented the most martyrs among the Hashemite families to Islam, and Ja'far was one of these shining stars.

On the day of Ashura, when the Imam's loyal companions were martyred, Ja'far, like his other brothers and cousins, eagerly prepared for martyrdom. He entered the battlefield with Imam Hussain's (AS) permission.

As he fought with his sword, he recited crushing epic poetry, introducing himself: 'I am the Abtahi youth from the family of Abu Talib, a family from the lineage of Hashim and Ghalib...'

Ja'far fought bravely against the Kufan army and, after killing 15 of Umar Sa'd's mercenaries, was finally martyred by 'Urwah ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami.'
quoteArabic: أَنَا الغُلامُ الأَبْطَحِيُّ الطّالِبِيُّ، مِنْ مَعْشَرٍ فِي هاشِمٍ وَغالِبِ...
quote: (In his war poetry:) I am the Abtahi Talibi youth, from a family of Hashim and Ghalib... and Hussain is the purest of the pure.
